metaTitle: The Boeing Atmosphere Global Documentation Hub - Icons

header.title: Icons
header.description: Use Atmosphere's icon library in order to bring consistency across products, create visual shortcuts, and improve scannability of content.

sections.text:
Boeing's icon library offers a collection of ready-made icons, saving designers time and ensuring visual consistency across products. The library uses Material Symbols Sharp icons as it's main base and reference. These guidelines will help you understand when and how to use the icon library.

Reach out to the design system team if you are interested in designing a unique icon not included in this library.

Material symbols

The Atmosphere icons library uses Material Symbols in the Sharp Style in the following combination:

Weight: 400
Filled: Off and On
Optical Size: 24

Sharp symbols display corners with straight edges, for a crisp style that remains legible even at smaller scales.

Pixel grid

Icons are aligned to the pixel grid to avoid and maintain sharpness across screen sizes and densities.

Lines that are not snapped to the pixel grid will be too anti-aliased, or grayed. A line that slightly enters a pixel will cause it to gray slightly, creating a "fuzzy" appearance. This is especially noticeable on devices with lower pixel densities. When designing with icons, always check the grid and conduct a pixel preview.

Keylines

Keylines are the grid and structure behind an iconography system. They help maintain consistency and guide the placement of common shapes as the icon library grows.

The diagram on the right shows the underlying keylines for a 24dp icon from Material 3's library.

Standard icon layout

Icon content should remain inside of the live area, which is the region of an image that is unlikely to be hidden from view (such as an area where sidebars appear upon scrolling).

If additional visual weight is needed, content may extend into the padding between the live area and the trim area (the complete size of a graphic). No parts of the icon should extend outside of the trim area.

images.text: For a 24dp icon, content should aim to stay within the live area (20dp x 20dp) with 2dp of padding.
No parts of the icon should extend outside of the trim area, the content may get cut off beyond the frame and create formatting inconsistency.

items: Select icons from the existing icons library
If you need an icon that is not available, submit a request to the Atmosphere team
Make sure to never resize an icon, this ensures consistency across all products

sections.text:
Sizes

Currently, our icon library has one size available, 24dp. Icons are intended to be used at 100% and should not be scaled up or down.

Refer to the icon library to see what icons are available, and contact the design system team if a new size is needed.

Icon buttons

Use icons that optically fit inside buttons. A good reference is to use an icon that is half the size of a button (e.g., a 24dp icon in a 48dp height button).

Learn more about icon buttons.

images.text: Adhere to the standard 24dp icon size. The icon library can be used to swap icon types, such as filled and unfilled options.
Do not scale icons or modify them. They are meant to be standard across all products. Custom variations will create communication inconsistencies.

sections.text:
Size and text relationship

For visual hierarchy, proper icon and text sizes should be maintained when placing these elements together on a single line. In most cases, a single line of text will be placed next to 24dp icons and are common within our badging system.

Medium and Large text may be used with 24dp icons. Small and Body text may be used with 24dp icons only when necessary.

Icons and text should be aligned to the vertical centers for this treatment. Use 8dp between 24dp icons and text.

Best practices

Fills

Some icons have an outline and a filled version. The filled versions are used to indicate a selected state or to increase their prominence within a hierarchy.

Aligment

Icons can be aligned top or center with a body of copy. The alignment depends on the height and density of the paired content. Icons align center for copy that spans 1-2 lines and align top for 3+ lines.

Color

Icons are monochromatic by default. The majority will use Interactive/Primary/Background/Default unless in a disabled state within a button. If an icon is paired with a label or body of text, the icon will match the color of the text.

Icons that convey semantic meaning, like attention, information, or confirmation, can use semantic colors over primary or secondary backgrounds. If the icon is over a background container using a semantic color, the icon will use an inverse color scheme instead.

Stroke width

All icons use a 2dp stroke width. This includes angles, curves, and negative space. In certain cases, smaller values like 1.5dp or 1dp are used to improve clarity.

Sharp icons

The Sharp icons from Material 3 have been selected as the standard. Most icons in this set have sharp corners, however some icons have details with curves and rounded edges. The lines and corner radius typically use 2dp, unless they include optical adjustments.

images.text: Adhere to sizing and alignment rules for single-line lockups as used within the badging system.
Don't pair icons with text sizes that appear visually too small or large, and don't align icons to only the cap height of text.

sections.text:
Support icons with a label

Use label text to support icons. Label text provides meaningful descriptions when symbols are more abstract.

Support the icon with a tooltip when labels can't be used.

images.text: Label text provides short descriptions, especially useful for navigation

Use caution if icons are displayed without labels. Icon meaning should always be unambiguous and accessible for all users. Text labels can be omitted in specific circumstances where reduced visual impact is necessary.

sections.text:
Target size

Adequate space should surround icons to ensure legibility and interaction. Symbols of 24dp should use a target size of 48dp.
